comment.body:
I acknowledged everything you are saying here. All I’m saying is there will be constantly new members coming in, so Alma showing that her analysis is practically applicable and successful is important (proof of concept). What isn’t true about that?

You’re very high strong man. Alma already told you once today to chill out and has done so in the past.

Also, the bought NVDA, AMD, and AAPL calls near their weekly high before it consolidated and corrected. Those were losing trades. She didn’t update them. Fair is fair. It does create confusion.

There’s some truth to your point that I may be putting too much emphasis on what she says practically with trading. But my logic is, that must be very important, as that represents her putting her money where her mouth is.

She has also said Monday or over the weekend that she would be looking to buy some NVDA calls on the NVDA “fakeout” decline that seemed to have occurred yesterday. She bought SPY calls as you alluded to, and you said she also bought NVDA calls. I was wondering what she was doing with that as she very explicit with communicating that. But I didn’t see that she actually bought yesterday, as it wasn’t updated in her thread.

At the end of the day, she agreed that she’s gotta tighten up her parameters as to what direct trading info she wishes to share and what she doesn’t. Floating in and out of discussing specific trades and positions leads to confusion when it isn’t updated as things occur. You already acknowledged that. Prime example is her views and trading with NVDA.

So how about, if you want to better understand what my intentions are with my last response, ask me “what I mean by it” instead of rushing to conclusions and freaking out, attaching meanings to what I’m saying.

I’ve been very delicate with my feedback and have acknowledged the many things Alma has done well.

comment.body:
When everyone was bearish, she said BTD.
When everyone was screaming moon, she said reversion, long puts.
And now, when everyone called for doomsday, she bought calls and broke it down for us perfectly.
Does this stuff just fly over your head, or what?
And what juggling, what proof of concept, man.
Lemme tell you something. In the industry, there are insanely good quant analysts who are terrible traders. But they predict what needs to be predicted, they calculate positioning, they lay out the structures. they just can’t execute trades for themselves.
What we take from the analysis is on us.
What if i trade pairs, or follow discretionary strategies? Alma’s analysis still helps.
We shouldn’t even go down this road, man.
The trading signal chat isn’t part of the service. She made that crystal clear on X.
Being a good trader yourself doesn’t mean she has to be one for you.

comment.body:
I am joining this chat class today, with the intention of seeking solutions rather than speaking in vain. Personally, I really like the threads that Alma creates every day, but sometimes newcomers like me miss information because people interrupt when she is speaking, and the thread gets fragmented. My idea would be:  

1. A channel with chat disabled on weekends for questions and answers that arose during the week or at the end of the day (as Alma sees fit).  
2. A channel with chat disabled for the threads that she deems necessary, so no one interrupts and the information remains visible to everyone. This way, Alma gets less exhausted, and we all benefit from having the information organized. (For these channels, the image she already has for Q&A is ready; we just need one that says "Thread" or simply the 🧵 emoji.)  
3. Another idea would be to try to answer questions within that message NOT using the reply option but instead using the alternative "REPLY IN A NEW SUBTHREAD." This way, I believe there would be fewer messages throughout the day, and the answers would remain within the same question or inquiry messages.  

Sorry for the long text.

comment.body:
If Alma is all by herself, in my opinion, the best thing she can do is:

1. Have “read only” chats instead of trading signals. This is where she’ll provide what she did on X (intraday in and out, intraday analysis, etc.)

2. The questions become too much during trading hours. Just close this chat in the AM after market opens. Open it around lunch and the final hour. After Alma’s thoughts are gathered and price does its thing throughout the day. Would probably help most people. Not just Alma

3. It seems a lot of people don’t understand definitions. I don’t know if it’s too much work or if someone else can do it for her. But, have a single post with links to videos on YouTube for the basics. I get this will take time. But it may cut down the repeat
